issues: ice palette skews cool against warm/charming brief — blues present but warmth is low
blade dot-hole detail reads as voids rather than ice-crystal facets, ambiguous at small scale
cross-guard white is flat — one highlight tone would lift the toy-diorama charm
reasoning: Emotional (60%): The bright cyan/sky-blue blade is saturated and magical — blues are explicitly in-palette for this Zelda brief. The design is not grim or scary, the brown handle adds warmth, and the overall silhouette reads as a friendly fantasy sword rather than anything menacing. However, an ice sword is inherently the coolest-toned item in the warm/charming Hyrule palette, and no compensating warm accent (gold trim, pink gem, warm highlight) bridges that gap. Emotional score ~7. Technical (40%): Four zoom panels confirm 1px hard outlines with no anti-aliasing, 2–3 tone hard shading on blade (dark blue outline, mid cyan, white specular strip), and pixel-grid-snapped construction throughout. The hand-pixeled quality is evident and the silhouette reads cleanly at the smallest zoom. The blade centre uses a column of square dots that appear as hollow voids rather than crystalline facets — technically clean but semantically weak. Technical score ~7. Weighted: 0.6×7 + 0.4×7 = 7.0. Sits solidly in marginal band; could pass with a warm accent (e.g. amber pommel gem) and replacing dot-voids with faceted highlight pixels.
